MASTER CALENDAR JURY ASBESTOS
Off Calendar Aug-23-2024 Case Settled - Set For Crc 3.1385 Hrg On Dec-05-24 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 301

MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
Off Calendar Jul-09-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Hearing re: INTERVENOR UNITED STATES FIDELITY & GUARANTY COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows:

Judge: Rochelle C. East. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Matter Not Reported. Court Reporter: Bailiff: Not Present. (301/RCE)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Jul-02-2024 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES Via Zoom:
Daniel J. Kelly of TUCKER ELLIS LLP (415-617-2222), appearing for defendant and moving party Continental Maritime of San Francisco Inc.
Kimberly J. Chu of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs and opposing parties Charles Aikins Jr, et al.

RULING:
Hearing held, argued, and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Continental Maritime of San Francisco Inc.'s Motion for Summary Judgment is DENIED. Opposition filed by Plaintiffs. Reply filed by Defendant. Defendant fails to sustain its initial burden of showing Plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain needed evidence of causation for the following reasons. (Aguilar v. Atl. Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826, 855.)
First, under California Code of Civil Procedure sec. 437c(f)(1), "A motion for summary adjudication shall be granted only if it completely disposes of a cause of action, an affirmative defense, a claim for damages, or an issue of duty." Defendant's motion does not seek to completely dispose of any claim. Instead, the motion merely seeks to limit the jury's assessment of damages. Defendant has not met this threshold with regard to their Motion for Summary Adjudication.

Defendant supports its Motion for Summary Judgment with limited discovery responses, but has not shown that it propounded special discovery. (See Defendant's Exhibit C.) The "Certificate of Death'' offered by Defendants does not completely foreclose asbestos-related disease as a cause of Decedent's death. (See Defendant's Exhibit B.) Similarly, the articles of incorporation do not meet the burden of showing no possible causation by Defendant. (See Defendant's Exhibits E, F, and G.) Order signed in open court. Judge: Rochelle C. East, Clerk: A. Mok. Not reported. (301/RCE)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, Alternatively, Summary Adjudication; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
Off Calendar Jul-02-2024 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES Via Zoom:
Alan J. Watson and Kathryn Richards of HOLLAND & KNIGHT LLP (213-896-2400 ext. 52512) appearing for defendant and moving party BAE Systems San Diego Ship Repair Inc.
Kimberly J. Chu of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs and opposing parties Charles Aikins Jr, et al.

RULING:
Hearing held, argued and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant BAE System's Motion for Summary Judgment, or in the alternative Summary Adjudication, is DENIED. Opposition filed by Plaintiffs. Reply filed by Defendant. Defendant fails to sustain its initial burden of showing that Plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain needed evidence of asbestos exposure and causation and that there is no triable issue of fact. (Aguilar v. Atl. Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826, 855.)

Defendant contends that Plaintiffs failed to show any link between BAE Systems and Decedent's injury because current discovery lacks an evidentiary link between Defendant and Decedent. But the Court finds that summary judgment cannot be granted where there was limited discovery, and "[a] motion for summary judgment is not a mechanism for rewarding limited discovery . . . ." (Weber v. John Crane, Inc., 143 Cal.App.4th 1433, 1442.) Defendant's special interrogatories did not seek all facts, all documents, and all witnesses; it merely sought information about the ships' names, dates, and the scope of Decedent's work on those ships. (Defense Exhibit C at 2-3.) Defendant has not deposed the witnesses Plaintiffs offered in its responses to general interrogatories. As such, Defendant's motion fails to address the full breadth of Plaintiffs' claims and fails to sustain its burden of showing that Plaintiffs do not possess, and cannot reasonably obtain, needed evidence to support their claims. Order signed in open court. Judge: Rochelle C. East, Clerk: A. Mok. Not reported. (301/RCE)
